summary: The client is carrying others’ emotional loads, struggles to ask for help, and tends to suppress negative emotions until a crash. This creates a cycle of high external demands (C01), difficulty with help-seeking (C02), emotion suppression (C03) followed by episodic breakdowns (C04), and growing resentment about being “the strong one” (C05) with limited boundary skills (C06). Targeted, modifiable levers across social, psychological, and interactional domains can disrupt this cycle by (1) tightening boundaries and reducing unwanted demands, (2) improving help-seeking and communication, (3) enhancing emotional awareness and processing, (4) establishing pre-crisis coping routines, and (5) supporting self-compassion and values-aligned role functioning. The proposed levers are designed to be measurable, feasible in typical care settings, and scalable over weeks to months without pharmacological or unsafe practices.

solutions.predictor_variable: Boundary-setting routine adoption (weekly boundary mapping and decline scripts)
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a structured boundary plan: (a) identify high-demand situations weekly, (b) generate 2-3 decline/redirect scripts, (c) set designated “no-ask” times (e.g., two 1-hour blocks daily). Use brief status updates (out-of-office or message templates) to communicate limits. Practice with a trusted friend or coach in role-plays, and track adherence vs. requests received.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly reduces external emotional labor (C01) by lowering unsolicited demands and improves ability to decline inappropriate requests (C06). This also preserves energy for personal needs and emotional processing.
solutions.measurement: Weekly count of boundary breaches avoided, number of declined requests, self-rated boundary efficacy (1–5), weekly energy level (0–10).
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for relationship strain; ensure decline scripts are respectful and non-judgmental. Adjust boundaries if there is consistent pushback or safety concerns.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: targets C01 (external demands) and C06 (boundary-setting skills).

solutions.solution_candidate: Create a 6-week plan to practice help-seeking: (a) identify 2–3 trusted people for distress support, (b) craft a 1-sentence ask tailored to each person, (c) schedule regular check-ins, (d) rehearse requests via role-play, (e) escalate to professional support if distress escalates. Build a simple “help-seeking kit” (text prompts, facilitator contacts).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces difficulty asking for help by providing concrete scripts and predictable pathways to support.
solutions.measurement: Number of times help is requested per week, perceived ease of asking (1–5), time-to-help-response from asked person (hours/days).
solutions.safety_notes: Encourage professional help if distress persists or worsens; ensure safe, voluntary help-seeking.

solutions.predictor_variable: Emotion labeling and processing routines (daily processing + weekly processing window)
solutions.solution_candidate: Incorporate a daily 5-minute emotion labeling/check-in (rating intensity and potential trigger) and a weekly 30-minute processing session (journaling or guided disclosure to a safe person). Provide an emotion-processing notebook or app. Include gentle mindfulness-based grounding as needed.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Counteracts chronic emotion suppression by increasing awareness and structured processing, reducing the buildup leading to crashes (C04).
solutions.measurement: Daily emotion labeling accuracy or completeness (0–100%), number of processing sessions per week, self-reported suppression score (1–5).
solutions.safety_notes: If processing triggers trauma memories, provide clinician-guided support or adapt to a non-traumatic processing format.

solutions.predictor_variable: Scheduled emotional processing to prevent crashes (pre-crisis coping plan)
solutions.solution_candidate: Create a weekly coping plan that includes (a) a 10–15 minute wind-down routine for emotional release, (b) a 1-page crisis plan with warning signs and quick coping steps, (c) a 1-hour social-support check-in window to decompress. Train in rapid mood-regulation techniques (breathing, grounding).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular emotional processing reduces episodic breakdowns after suppression by providing predictable outlets.
solutions.measurement: Crash episodes per week, duration of mood dips, utilization of coping plan components (yes/no).
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for rumination; adjust to avoid over-fixation on distress.

solutions.predictor_variable: Role fatigue mitigation via delegation and values-aligned planning
solutions.solution_candidate: Lead a values-clarification session to identify core roles and acceptable boundaries, create a delegation map (which tasks to reassign or share), and schedule regular check-ins to adjust load. Include self-compassion prompts to reduce resentment.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Addresses fatigue and resentment by aligning duties with core values, sharing load, and reducing unnecessary burdens.
solutions.measurement: Self-reported resentment (1–5) and role satisfaction (1–5), number of tasks delegated per week, perceived boundary stability.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure no coercion; maintain healthy relationships; monitor for over-delegation and caregiver burnout in others.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: targets C05 (resentment) and C01/C06 (demands and boundaries).

solutions.solution_candidate: Introduce a daily self-compassion practice (brief compassionate self-talk, brief cognitive reframe) and a weekly boundary-review session to ensure boundaries remain sustainable. Include a personal “inner ally” exercise to reduce guilt in setting limits.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces guilt/shame and strengthens ongoing boundary maintenance, supporting sustainable role functioning.
solutions.measurement: Self-compassion scores (brief scale), boundary-maintenance score, frequency of self-care acts.
solutions.safety_notes: Watch for perfectionism; ensure self-compassion practice is grounded in realistic self-expectations.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: targets C05 (resentment) and C06 (boundary skills).
